meta.title: Box
meta.description: Box is a basic layout component that serves as a wrapper or container. It's based on the Chakra UI Box component and supports all style props for rapid UI development.

mdx:
## Import

```jsx
import { Box } from "@commercetools/nimbus";
```

## Usage

### Basic usage

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Box p="100" bg="primary.3">
    Basic Box
  </Box>
);
```

### As a flex container

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Box display="flex" gap="100">
    <Box p="100" bg="primary.3">
      Item 1
    </Box>
    <Box p="100" bg="primary.3">
      Item 2
    </Box>
  </Box>
);
```

### With border

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Box p="100" border="solid-50" borderColor="primary.3">
    Box with border
  </Box>
);
```

### With shadow

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Box p="100" boxShadow="6">
    Box with shadow
  </Box>
);
```

### With asChild

Use the `asChild` prop to apply Box styles to any child element while
maintaining the child's functionality:

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Box p="100" bg="primary.3" asChild>
    <button onClick={() => alert("Clicked!")}>
      I am a button with Box styles
    </button>
  </Box>
);
```

## Import

```jsx
import { Box } from "@commercetools/nimbus";
```

## Usage

### Basic usage

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Box p="100" bg="primary.3">
    Basic Box
  </Box>
);
```

### As a flex container

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Box display="flex" gap="100">
    <Box p="100" bg="primary.3">
      Item 1
    </Box>
    <Box p="100" bg="primary.3">
      Item 2
    </Box>
  </Box>
);
```

### With border

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Box p="100" border="solid-50" borderColor="primary.3">
    Box with border
  </Box>
);
```

### With shadow

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Box p="100" boxShadow="6">
    Box with shadow
  </Box>
);
```

### With asChild

Use the `asChild` prop to apply Box styles to any child element while
maintaining the child's functionality:

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Box p="100" bg="primary.3" asChild>
    <button onClick={() => alert("Clicked!")}>
      I am a button with Box styles
    </button>
  </Box>
);
```

a11y.mdx:
## Accessibility

Accessibility ensures that digital content and functionality are usable by
everyone, including people with disabilities, by addressing visual, auditory,
cognitive, and physical limitations.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Box as="section" p="100">
    Section content
  </Box>
);
```

### Accessibility standards

- **Use semantic HTML:** Box renders a `div` by default but can be changed to
  any other HTML element using the `as` prop. Choose appropriate semantic
  elements (e.g., `section`, `article`, `nav`) to provide meaningful structure.
- **Maintain proper document structure:** Ensure the chosen element maintains
  logical document hierarchy and semantic meaning.
- **Avoid layout-only divs when possible:** When the Box has semantic meaning,
  use the `as` prop to render the appropriate HTML element.

dev.mdx:
## Getting started ### Import ```tsx import { Box, type BoxProps } from '@commercetools/nimbus'; ``` ### Basic usage The Box component is a fundamental layout primitive that accepts all Chakra UI system props for styling: ```jsx live-dev const App = () => ( Basic Box ) ``` ## Usage examples ### Spacing and layout Use spacing tokens to control padding and margin: ```jsx live-dev const App = () => ( Padding: 100 Padding: 200 Padding: 300 ) ``` ### Colors and backgrounds Apply background colors and text colors using design tokens: ```jsx live-dev const App = () => ( Primary colors Positive colors Critical colors ) ``` ### Borders and shadows Create visual depth with borders and shadows: ```jsx live-dev const App = () => ( Box with border Box with shadow Border, radius, and shadow ) ``` ### As a flex container Use Flexbox props to create flexible layouts in both horizontal and vertical orientations: ```jsx live-dev const App = () => ( Column 1 Column 2 Column 3 Row 1 Row 2 Row 3 ) ``` **When to use Flexbox:** - One-dimensional layouts (row OR column) - Aligning and distributing items along a single axis - Components that need to grow or shrink based on content - Navigation bars, button groups, form layouts > [!TIP]\ > See the [Flex](/components/layout/flex) component documentation for complete API reference and advanced usage. ### As a grid container Create two-dimensional grid layouts with Grid props. Grid is ideal for complex layouts where you need control over both rows and columns simultaneously: ```jsx live-dev const App = () => ( Grid Item 1 Grid Item 2 Grid Item 3 Grid Item 4 Grid Item 5 Grid Item 6 ) ``` **When to use Grid:** - Two-dimensional layouts (rows AND columns) - Complex layouts with defined grid structures - Card grids, dashboards, form layouts with multiple columns - When you need precise control over item placement > [!TIP]\ > See the [Grid](/components/layout/grid) component documentation for complete API reference and advanced usage. ### Polymorphic rendering Change the underlying HTML element using the `as` prop: ```jsx live-dev const App = () => ( Rendered as section Rendered as article Rendered as nav ) ``` ### Composition with asChild Apply Box styles to interactive elements (buttons, links, inputs, etc.) while maintaining their native functionality. The `asChild` prop merges Box's styling props directly onto the child element instead of wrapping it in an additional `
`, which helps preserve semantic HTML structure and accessibility. This is particularly important for interactive elements where the element type must remain unchanged for proper behavior and accessibility. **Benefits:** - **No extra DOM wrapper**: Styles are applied directly to the child element - **Semantic HTML**: Maintains proper HTML structure (e.g., a ` e.preventDefault()}> Link with Box styles ) ``` ## Component requirements ## Accessibility The Box component is a layout primitive and renders as a `div` by default. For proper accessibility: - **Semantic HTML**: Use the `as` prop to render appropriate semantic elements (`section`, `article`, `nav`, `aside`, etc.) based on the content's purpose - **Landmarks**: When using Box as a landmark element (like `nav` or `main`), provide an `aria-label` for screen readers if multiple landmarks of the same type exist on the page - **Interactive elements**: When using `asChild` with interactive elements (buttons, links), ensure the child element has proper accessibility attributes ```tsx // Semantic section

Section Title

 // Navigation landmark {/* navigation items */} // Interactive element with asChild ``` If your use case requires tracking and analytics for this component, it is good practice to add a **persistent**, **unique** id to the component: ```tsx const PERSISTENT_ID = "example-box"; export const Example = () => ( Content ); ``` #### Keyboard navigation Box is a non-interactive layout component and does not support keyboard navigation by default. However, when using the `asChild` prop with interactive elements, the child element's keyboard interactions are preserved. ## API reference ## Common patterns ### Card-like containers Create card-style containers with borders, shadows, and rounded corners: ```jsx live-dev const App = () => ( Card Title This is a card-like container built with Box component using border, shadow, and border radius. Colored Card A card with background color and larger shadow for emphasis. ) ``` > [!TIP]\ > See the [Card](/components/card) component documentation for complete API reference and advanced usage. ### Responsive layouts Use responsive object syntax for adaptive layouts across breakpoints: ```jsx live-dev const App = () => ( Item 1 Item 2 Item 3 Item 4 Item 5 Item 6 ) ``` ### Centered content container Create a centered container with maximum width: ```jsx live-dev const App = () => ( Centered Container This content is centered with a maximum width, commonly used for readable text layouts or contained content areas. ) ``` ## Testing your implementation These examples demonstrate how to test your implementation when using Box within your application. As the component's internal functionality is already tested by Nimbus, these patterns help you verify your integration and application-specific logic. ### Basic Rendering Tests Verify the component renders with expected content ```tsx import { describe, it, expect } from "vitest"; import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react"; import { Box, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus"; describe("Box - Basic rendering", () => { it("renders content correctly", () => { render( Box content ); expect(screen.getByText("Box content")).toBeInTheDocument(); }); it("renders with children components", () => { render( Child element ); expect(screen.getByText("Child element")).toBeInTheDocument(); }); }); ``` ### Style Props Tests Test rendering with various Chakra UI style props ```tsx import { describe, it, expect } from "vitest"; import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react"; import { Box, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus"; describe("Box - Style props", () => { it("applies custom className", () => { render( Styled Box ); const box = screen.getByText("Styled Box"); expect(box).toHaveClass("custom-class"); }); it("renders with data attributes for testing", () => { render( Test Box ); expect(screen.getByTestId("test-box")).toBeInTheDocument(); }); }); ``` ### Polymorphic Rendering Tests Test rendering as different HTML elements ```tsx import { describe, it, expect } from "vitest"; import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react"; import { Box, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus"; describe("Box - Polymorphic rendering", () => { it("renders as section element", () => { render( Section content ); const section = screen.getByRole("region", { name: "Test section" }); expect(section).toBeInTheDocument(); expect(section.tagName).toBe("SECTION"); }); it("renders as nav element", () => { render( Navigation content ); const nav = screen.getByRole("navigation", { name: "Test navigation" }); expect(nav).toBeInTheDocument(); expect(nav.tagName).toBe("NAV"); }); it("renders as article element", () => { render( Article content ); const article = screen.getByRole("article"); expect(article).toBeInTheDocument(); expect(article.tagName).toBe("ARTICLE"); }); }); ``` ### AsChild Composition Tests Test style composition with asChild prop ```tsx import { describe, it, expect } from "vitest"; import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react"; import { Box, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus"; describe("Box - AsChild composition", () => { it("applies styles to child button element", () => { render( ); const button = screen.getByRole("button", { name: "Styled Button" }); expect(button).toBeInTheDocument(); expect(button.tagName).toBe("BUTTON"); }); it("preserves child link functionality with styles", () => { render( Styled Link ); const link = screen.getByRole("link", { name: "Styled Link" }); expect(link).toBeInTheDocument(); expect(link).toHaveAttribute("href", "/test"); }); }); ``` ## Resources - [Storybook](https://nimbus-storybook.vercel.app/?path=/docs/components-box--docs) - [Chakra UI Style Props](https://v2.chakra-ui.com/docs/styled-system/style-props)
